chapter: Chapter 12: Magnetic Effects of Electric Current

questions.question: How can the presence of a magnetic field around a current-carrying conductor be detected?
questions.answer: By observing the deflection of a compass needle placed near the conductor.

questions.question: What happens to the direction of magnetic field lines if the direction of current is reversed?
questions.answer: The direction of the magnetic field lines also reverses.

questions.question: Why do magnetic field lines never intersect each other?
questions.answer: Because at a point the magnetic field can have only one direction.

questions.question: What does the closeness of magnetic field lines indicate?
questions.answer: It indicates the strength of the magnetic field.

questions.question: How does increasing the current affect the magnetic field around a conductor?
questions.answer: The strength of the magnetic field increases.

questions.question: Why does a solenoid behave like a bar magnet?
questions.answer: Because the magnetic field pattern around it is similar to that of a bar magnet.

questions.question: How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased?
questions.answer: By increasing the current, increasing the number of turns, or using a soft iron core.

questions.question: What happens when a current-carrying conductor is placed parallel to a magnetic field?
questions.answer: No magnetic force acts on the conductor.

questions.question: In which direction does the force act on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field?
questions.answer: Perpendicular to both the direction of current and magnetic field.

questions.question: Why is Fleming's left-hand rule useful?
questions.answer: It helps determine the direction of force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field.

questions.question: Why is a soft iron core used in electromagnets?
questions.answer: Because it becomes strongly magnetised and loses magnetism quickly when current stops.

questions.question: What would happen if the split ring in an electric motor was replaced by a complete ring?
questions.answer: The current would not reverse and the motor would not rotate continuously.

questions.question: What causes the coil of an electric motor to rotate?
questions.answer: The magnetic force acting on the current-carrying coil placed in a magnetic field.

questions.question: How can electric current be induced in a coil?
questions.answer: By changing the magnetic field around the coil.

questions.question: What happens to the induced current if the magnet is moved faster inside a coil?
questions.answer: The magnitude of induced current increases.

questions.question: What is the role of carbon brushes in a generator?
questions.answer: They maintain electrical contact between the rotating coil and external circuit.

questions.question: Why does induced current change direction in an AC generator?
questions.answer: Because the direction of motion of the coil changes periodically.

questions.question: Why are electric motors widely used in household appliances?
questions.answer: Because they efficiently convert electrical energy into mechanical energy.

questions.question: Why should a fuse be connected in series in a circuit?
questions.answer: So that the entire current passes through it and it melts when excessive current flows.

questions.question: Why is the earth wire thicker in household circuits?
questions.answer: To safely carry large fault currents to the ground.

questions.question: What will happen if too many appliances are connected to one socket?
questions.answer: Overloading occurs and excessive current flows in the circuit.

questions.question: Why should electrical appliances be properly earthed?
questions.answer: To prevent electric shock due to leakage current.

questions.question: How does a generator differ from an electric motor?
questions.answer: A generator converts mechanical energy to electrical energy, while a motor converts electrical energy to mechanical energy.

questions.question: What happens when the current in a solenoid is switched off?
questions.answer: The magnetic field produced by the solenoid disappears.

questions.question: Why are magnetic field lines drawn closer near the poles of a magnet?
questions.answer: Because the magnetic field is strongest near the poles.
